issues.title: Setting delegateAgent to a different agent than acpAgent crashes master process (wta-master pipe connect failure)
issues.next_action_reason: Crash without Feedback Hub diagnostics; T3 drafted, with a WTA-log PS for the master-pipe startup path. Suggested labels should replace Needs-Tag-Fix.
issues.suggested_reply:
Sorry you hit a crash @Aust-aa-36. For crashes specifically we need the backend diagnostic data, which you can capture via Feedback Hub: https://aka.ms/intelligent-terminal-feedback. Pick the matching category, hit "Share My Feedback" after submitting, and paste the link here.

The report already has a strong repro and the concrete error `after 21 attempt(s): The system cannot find the file specified. (os error 2)`, which is useful context. Without Feedback Hub data we cannot correlate the master-process crash to a specific stack trace.

PS: because this involves the helper to wta-master startup path, a zipped WTA log folder would also help: `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Packages\*IntelligentTerminal*\LocalCache\Local\IntelligentTerminal\logs\` (or `%LOCALAPPDATA%\IntelligentTerminal\logs\` for unpackaged dev builds). If it is hard to reproduce, set `WTA_LOG=debug` before launching.

issues.title: Stucked on loading

Hi @MartinSusky, thanks for the report. The screenshot shows the agent pane stuck at <!-- from image -->Loading...<!-- /from image -->, and your follow-up says <!-- from comment -->it's been an hour and it's still loading<!-- /from comment -->, so this is beyond the expected first-run delay.

Could you grab a WTA log next time you hit this?

1. Reproduce the issue.
2. Zip `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Packages\*IntelligentTerminal*\LocalCache\Local\IntelligentTerminal\logs\` (or `%LOCALAPPDATA%\IntelligentTerminal\logs\` for unpackaged dev builds).
3. Attach the zip here.

If the issue is hard to reproduce, set `WTA_LOG=debug` first to get the noisier traces.

issues.title: Command detection handling on PowerShell 7 not working with regex
issues.next_action_reason: Autofix/shell-integration bug has version and repro outline, but needs WTA logs and the exact PowerShell 7 regex command before engineering can isolate the detection path.
issues.suggested_reply:
Hi @hamza-usmani, could you grab a WTA log next time you hit this?

I see this is specific to <!-- from body -->Works in PS5 but not PS7 due to how regex is handled in the shells differently<!-- /from body -->, so the log plus the exact regex command should tell us whether the PS7 hook is missing the failed command marker or whether Autofix is filtering it out.

1. Reproduce the issue in the PowerShell 7 profile.
2. Zip `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Packages\*IntelligentTerminal*\LocalCache\Local\IntelligentTerminal\logs\` (or `%LOCALAPPDATA%\IntelligentTerminal\logs\` for unpackaged dev builds).
3. Attach the zip here.

If the issue is hard to reproduce, set `WTA_LOG=debug` first to get the noisier traces.
